title: Can not see a direct way to pass module scope to an external function
body:
---


Author Name: **Arthur Kahlich**
Original Redmine Issue: 972 from https://www.veripool.org
Original Date: 2015-10-01


---

This is in the context of a function call made with
```
$c("func(int foo, ",<some form of module scope>, ", int bar);");

```
where what I want is either a text string of the form:
```
v.top.middle.current_module_instance

```
or a vpiHandle for current_module_instance.

One application of this is the implementation of direct memory access backdoors for a debugger interface, where the function implements an instance of memory model with a backdoor to the debugger independent of the normal signals controlling the memory when it is embodied as hardware. A design will have several instances of the same memory and ultimately a different vpiHandle needs to be passed for each one.

So far the only method I have come up with that uses an *existing release* of Verilator involves searching for each address in a reverse-lookup of the public signals. Yes, I could do the reverse maps, but that seems hideous compared to what could be done.

For instance, Verilator implements __FILE__ and __LINE__ reserved words. One possible solution to my problem would be implementing something like __MODULE__, defined to be the same string as that printed by $display("%M");

Another solution would be to have svGetScope() return a module scope rather than the scope of the current Verilator class.

Am I missing something?
